candidate_summary: NGATU-LAMINATE quantitative scaling-law prediction P̂(L, θ, σ²) = α·L^β·sin(θ)·σ^γ, fitting downstream LLM performance using layer-count + inter-layer init-orthogonality + residual-stream variance as predictor features via calibration on small set of trained models.

results_analysis.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models (Kaplan)
results_analysis.note: Kaplan scaling law — parametric power-law calibration prediction. Same form/method, different features. Triple hit.

results_analysis.title: Scaling Laws Meet Model Architecture (Inference-Efficient LLMs)
results_analysis.note: Architecture-aware scaling-law unification — feature-augmented scaling laws covering same prediction paradigm. Triple hit.

results_analysis.title: Generalizing Scaling Laws for Dense and Sparse LLMs
results_analysis.note: Generalized scaling-law framework for dense+sparse — same parametric calibration-fit prediction. Triple hit.

results_analysis.title: Commutative Scaling of Width and Depth (JMLR)
results_analysis.note: Commutative (essentially ORTHOGONAL) width-depth scaling — orthogonality of depth dimension already established as scaling feature. Triple hit; directly attacks the novelty axis.

results_analysis.title: Scaling Laws via Feature Learning Dynamics
results_analysis.note: Depth-aware LR scaling via feature-learning dynamics; depth is a key predictor and residual-stream variance is the measured collapse signal. Triple hit.

results_analysis.title: LLM Scaling Laws GPT-3 to o3
results_analysis.note: Survey of LLM scaling laws — kw and sem hits even though func below threshold.

results_analysis.title: How to build AI scaling laws (MIT 2025)
results_analysis.note: 485 pretrained models, 1.9M metrics, 1000+ scaling-law fits — exact calibration-fit methodology NGATU proposes. Triple hit.

results_analysis.title: Scaling Laws Meet Architecture (duplicate)
results_analysis.note: Duplicate of rank 2; same paper. Triple hit.

verdict_sentence: Candidate FAILS novelty: 8 of 8 retrieved results hit (kw>=2, sem>=0.7, func>=0.7) — Kaplan scaling law, commutative width-depth orthogonality (JMLR), generalized dense+sparse scaling, architecture-aware scaling, MIT 1000+ scaling-law calibration methodology, and feature-learning depth-aware scaling all jointly cover the LLM-scaling-law-with-depth-and-orthogonality-and-residual-feature space, leaving no room for the proposed orthogonality-as-predictor novelty axis.
